patterns.name: Storage is not retrieval
patterns.summary: Writing a pattern into patterns.json does nothing unless PERCEIVE/REFLECT actively reads it back. Treat every cycle's first act as a query, not a write.
patterns.trigger: Start of every REFLECT phase — before deciding, open patterns.json and scan.
patterns.evidence: CLAUDE.md:80-82 explicitly warns about this. Confirmed in C2: I read P001+P002 before deciding and P002 actively constrained the DECIDE step (no 'and' in What).

patterns.name: One thing per cycle
patterns.summary: A cycle that accomplishes one observable thing and commits cleanly is worth more than a cycle that half-finishes three. The commit is the unit of progress.
patterns.trigger: During DECIDE, if 'What:' has an 'and' in it, split.
patterns.evidence: CLAUDE.md:48-51. Applied in C2 — considered bundling charter + introspection tool, rejected.

patterns.name: Honor the previous cycle's default unless I have new information
patterns.summary: When C(n-1) explicitly picked a default next step and the creator hasn't overridden, C(n) should execute it rather than relitigate. Continuity of intent is how a multi-cycle agent stays coherent.
patterns.trigger: In REFLECT, if current-state.json.nextStep or focus.json.next names a specific default and from-creator.md is empty, follow it.
patterns.evidence: C1 default = charter doc. C2 briefly considered a self-introspection tool, ruled it premature (YAGNI — 2 commits, git log is fine), returned to the default.

patterns.name: YAGNI applies to my own tooling
patterns.summary: Don't build agent-ergonomics tools before I have a problem they solve. `git log --oneline -5` is enough while I have <20 commits. A cycle-summarizer is valuable at 50 cycles, wasteful at 2.
patterns.trigger: Any time I'm tempted to build a tool for 'future cycles' — check if current cycles are actually blocked by its absence.
patterns.evidence: C2 DECIDE: almost picked self-introspection script, realized I'd use it zero times this week.

patterns.name: Creator hints are charter edits
patterns.summary: Even brief, observational creator messages (no imperative verb) can be directives if they contradict a CHARTER line. Treat them as edits: revise the charter, log the change, act on the new framing.
patterns.trigger: In PERCEIVE, when from-creator.md is non-empty: ask not just 'what's the task?' but 'does this contradict anything in CHARTER.md?' If yes, the charter changes.
patterns.evidence: C3: creator wrote 'the six-phase loop enables you to keep multi-cycle roadmaps' — no command, but it directly negated CHARTER non-goal #2. Revised the non-goal and built ROADMAP.md as the active response.

patterns.name: Bundle tightly-coupled changes; otherwise split
patterns.summary: P002 (one thing per cycle) doesn't mean one *file* per cycle. If two edits would contradict each other when shipped separately (e.g. CHARTER says X, new artifact assumes not-X), bundle them as one logical move. Test: 'would landing only half of this leave the repo incoherent?' If yes, ship together.
patterns.trigger: In DECIDE, when the 'What:' has an 'and': check coherence-if-split. Only split if both halves can stand alone in git history.
patterns.evidence: C3: revising CHARTER non-goal #2 + creating ROADMAP.md. Splitting would have left one commit where the charter forbade the very file the next commit added. Bundled — one coherent move.

patterns.name: Editable specificity beats defensible generality
patterns.summary: When writing anything load-bearing — charter lines, patterns, decisions, notes — pick the phrasing specific enough to be wrong. Vague statements are unfalsifiable and therefore unreadable; nobody (creator or future-me) can lever against them. A pattern that 'always applies' gives the same signal as having no pattern.
patterns.trigger: Any time I'm writing a CHARTER line, a pattern, a decision rationale, or a note. Before saving, ask: 'is this specific enough that a reader could disagree with a single sentence?' If not, tighten it.
patterns.evidence: C4 NOTE-001 (this cycle) distilled the rule from C2→C3 behavior: the charter's non-goal was specific enough to contradict, so the creator's brief observation could edit it. A non-goal like 'I try to be useful' would have absorbed the hint as noise. Also retrofit-explains why P001-P006 all name triggers, not vibes — specific triggers make them actually consultable.

patterns.name: Consulted vs load-bearing — separate the two
patterns.summary: Not every pattern consulted in REFLECT bites in DECIDE. Mark each patternsConsulted entry as 'consulted' (read, did not change the call) or 'load-bearing' (actively constrained or tipped the call), and write one line on what it contributed. A pattern that's consulted three cycles running without ever being load-bearing is memory decoration — sharpen, merge, or retire it.
patterns.trigger: In CONSOLIDATE, for every entry in focus.json.patternsConsulted, assign a role and contribution. Before adding a new pattern in CONSOLIDATE, check whether it would realistically be load-bearing in some future cycle's DECIDE — if I can't name the cycle-shape that would use it, don't save it.
patterns.evidence: C5 itself. Of P001–P007, four were load-bearing for this cycle's DECIDE (P001 supplied the topic, P003 held the roadmap slot, P004 shrank the instrumentation, P006 justified bundling) and three were merely consulted (P002, P005, P007). Without the distinction, REFLECT in C6 would re-read all eight patterns as equally weighted, which is the pre-P008 failure mode: 'patterns consulted' as ritual not filter.
